element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Multi-use tool for jumping chasms, killing dinosaurs, holding off enemies, passing between humans
Reaching high ledges through cooperative positioning
Primary vertical traversal mechanism for tribe
Will eat unarmed humans in walking range; avoidable or spear-killable
Uncontrollable but usable ally for reaching otherwise unreachable platforms
Specialized late-game tool with unique applications
Hostile NPCs with weapons, higher threat level than dinosaurs

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully reaching a colored goal tile
Acquiring a new tool or ability
typical_tension_triggers: Humans dying and replacements needed from tribe pool
Approaching minimum required humans threshold for level completion
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation with independent level-based challenges; tension derives from resource management (tribe population) and time pressure rather than continuous threat

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Rescue opportunities to add additional humans to tribe (optional side objectives)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of spear and stacking mechanics in relatively safe contexts; learning basic tool use and human switching
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Additional tools introduced (torch, wheel, rope); dinosaur enemies become more threatening; pterodactyl riding adds complexity; time pressure increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Witch doctor tool; spear-wielding enemy tribe members; multi-tool puzzle solutions required; tribe population management becomes critical
notable_spikes: First dinosaur encounter requiring combat/evasion strategy
Introduction of enemy tribe members (higher threat than dinosaurs)
Levels requiring precise tool combinations and tribe coordination

notable_level_patterns.description: Early levels focused on introducing and mastering single tools before combining them in later puzzles
Each level has independent objectives: reach end tile, collect specific tool, kill certain number of dinosaurs, or other survival-based goals
Death of humans creates resource scarcity; rescue missions offer optional expansion; game over triggers when active humans fall below level minimum
Mid-to-late game introduces uncontrollable but advantageous NPCs (pterodactyl) requiring spatial positioning and timing
